ops.value: Total CLI commands captured in the production filter (excludes e2e-* tags).
Root-span count for `dot deploy`. Drops if telemetry is disabled or the binary version is too old to instrument deploy.
Hard failures the tool itself broke (cli.status:error AND cli.expected:false). Expected user-input failures (invalid domain, missing PoP) are excluded — those live in the Failures Detail dashboard.

Percentage of commands that emitted any captureWarning or hard error. The propagation path is verified (telemetry.test.ts). Most production warnings occur on runs that fail; gap widens as retries succeed cleanly.
Average wall-clock duration of root spans, in ms. Slow trends usually point at chain RPC latency.
count_unique(cli.repo) for the production filter. Watch for unexpected churn — a sudden drop suggests internal-org regex changes or telemetry being disabled in CI.
Per-command, per-source breakdown. CI vs local activity is the most useful split here. If self-hosted runner volume drops without an org change, suspect runner naming.
p50/p95 of command duration, split by source. The CI/local divergence reflects testnet vs local chain RPC latency.
Command volume over time. Use this to confirm a release shipped (a step-up in volume after release date) and to spot drop-offs.
Three-way split: success, expected failure, unexpected failure. Expected failures are user-input mistakes (the spec calls these isExpectedCliError matches). Unexpected failures are bugs.
Phase breakdown for cli.deploy.*. Order by avg duration to find which phase is the bottleneck. Storage-and-DotNS dominates by design — chunk uploads + DotNS commit-reveal both span seconds.
Deploy options matrix — combinations of mode/playground/modable/contracts. Useful to find a regression that only appears in one combination.

Sub-phase breakdown of the playground publish — metadata upload vs registry contract publish. Registry publish has a 3-attempt retry loop; high counts here without proportional warning events suggest the retry path is not hitting captureWarning.
Top error messages grouped by phase. Use this to prioritise expected-error copy improvements (cli.expected:true) vs bug fixes (cli.expected:false).
Most recent failures. Use the timestamp + repo to pull the offending PR or runner.
All warnings in the playground-cli project, excluding e2e-* tagged runs. Each row is a unique warning title — captureWarning callsites should keep titles stable so this groups cleanly.
Warnings over time, automatically grouped by title (no hard-coded list). Any new captureWarning string will surface here without a dashboard change.
Most recent commands across the production filter. Useful for ad-hoc 'what did this PR do' lookups by repo + timestamp.
